title: 命案目睹记
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\136-命案目睹记-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Detective Caddock visits Alfred at his modern, stylishly decorated house in Hampstead to investigate a murder. Caddock suspects a tall, dark-haired man with connections to Rutherford Hall, but he seeks Alfred to gather information. During the interview, Alfred claims he cannot remember his activities on Friday, December 20th, though he remembers celebrating Christmas with his father in Blakenhampton. Alfred expresses jealousy toward his brother Harold, whom he describes as the successful pride of the family. He also dismisses the medical concerns of a doctor named Camper regarding their father's health.

As the questioning continues, Alfred inadvertently reveals he knows the murder occurred in a long warehouse. He attempts to deflect suspicion by claiming he was in Leeds at a hotel near the town hall on that Friday, though he cannot recall the hotel's name. Alfred argues he has no motive to kill the deceased woman, even if she were Edmund's widow, suggesting the family would rather provide financial maintenance for the boy. 

Following the interview, a man named Mr. Weatherall informs Mr. Crockford that he has identified a man involved in Dicky Rogers' canned food business who is now connected to smuggling watches and Italian goods in Soho. This realization helps Mr. Crockford understand why Alfred seemed familiar; Alfred has been involved in minor smuggling operations.
